description: Wilhelm is drafted into the Wehrmacht for short training with 8. Battr./Art. Regt. 256 (later 2./844), quartered in barracks.
First night of the deployment as the 3rd wave in the Polish campaign; after a short midday arrival, vehicles were parked in the yard and everyone went to bed early.
After driving via Prague and Königgrätz, quartered in a former Czech barracks, sleeping on straw, barracks drill and exercises in the field. The local population very passive and reserved.
After 2 days and nights of rail transport, arrived in the west via the Rhine at Rüdesheim; further training at the military training grounds.
New garrison, peacetime-style duty. Marianne and Frau Fankhänel and Frau Behr visit. Getting noticed and punitive drill. Lots of pub time with Skat, chess, and billiards.
After departing Oggersheim at midnight and a dreadfully cold ride, one night in a private billet with very kind people.
Tuesday drove toward Westphalia, by evening took quarters near Paderborn; reception by the local population was poor.

Arrived Wednesday afternoon at the new destination, 'stockkath.' (strongly Catholic). Private billet at Küdde's with Auer, Bachinger and Müller. Chimneys full of sausage and ham, the locals stingy. Promoted to Oberkanonier.
Quartered in a grand hotel, straw piled up in the hall, about 30 km from Dortmund, a winter-sports stay. Wilhelm spends New Year's and January here.
Short medical-orderly course with Meinel and Max, comfortable duty but poor quarters.
Back in Nottuln; now lodges with a worker in the housing estate with 6 children, later at the Schürmanns' with full board. Now drives the battery officer in the Kübelwagen.

place: Village on the Dutch border (near Borken)
description: Position change, on a farm a quarter-hour from the orderly room. Unfair treatment by the new major, SS company next door, sharp duty, ammunition is being loaded up.
Easter spent with forest runs and Skat; flak can be heard from the industrial area. Exercise maneuvers with all the trimmings. Telephone Weseke 268.
The nice quarters in Weseke traded for a modest bed of straw. Helga is born on 1940-05-07 in Chemnitz.
As Army Artillery with the 1st Panzer Army, crossed the Dutch border at Elten together with the SS — beginning of the Western Campaign.
First time taking up a firing position, bombardment of Fort Westerfort. After crossing the IJssel, continued on at great speed.
Advance came to a halt at the Grebbe Line; fired almost 3 days without pause — 1000 tons of shells and 30 Stukas break the position.
On the last day before the capitulation, the entire 11th Battery formed a shock troop up to 10 km from Utrecht, facing a Dutch brigade which surrenders.
3 days' rest after the Holland fighting.
One day in Turnhout.
In a beautiful North Sea resort; the 11th Battery sinks a picket boat. Limber position; haven't seen the sea yet.
3 days near Antwerp; after 5 hours of sleep straight into the firing position.
Firing position, beginning of the Battle of Flanders. Advanced about 8 km daily; shelling of Thielt, Gynze, Diksmuide and Dunkirk.
Days of major fighting before Dunkirk, the English are withdrawing. Observation and shelling, fabulous air battles with Messerschmitts. Took part in the Lys crossing.
After driving via Ypres, Lille, Douai to Arras for rest near Essen. Recover with food, sleep, football, Skat and music.
Resting. Everyone slaughters and roasts chickens, pigeons and hares; the field kitchen takes a calf and a pig. Then march via Roye, Montdidier, Beauvais toward Paris.
The IV./AR 256 is among the first troops to march into Paris, a grand parade on the Place de la Concorde.
With a division's vanguard pushed almost to Orléans, briefly deployed as an infantry shock troop, took 300 prisoners. Lt. Kästner killed in action.
After day-marches of 160-200 km via Versailles and Chartres to Angers. Fired for the last time there; Wilhelm has a fever and diarrhea.
Were supposed to go to Biarritz, then re-routed. Waiting time in dumb cow-towns. Wilhelm is promoted to Gefreiter.
Transferred to nice quarters, town about the size of Glauchau. Strolls through town, cinema, lunch — finally something civilized. In the cafés, pretty, heavily made-up girls.

place: Sillé-le-Guillaume ('Wilhelmsruhe', 40 km N of Le Mans)
description: Via Thouars, Saumur, La Flèche, Le Mans, transferred to an old castle, later moved into a barracks camp (forest camp) with bathing facilities.
Wilhelm is on his first leave since the beginning of the war, with Marianne, and meets his daughter Helga for the first time.
After leave, 24 hours in a 2nd-class compartment via Luxembourg to Argentan, then to quarters. Spa town Bagnoles nearby. End of October, main dressing station with guard duty.
About 200 km west of Tessé for live-fire exercises at the training grounds. Wilhelm as ammunition driver without much to do.

Early in the morning, 200 km north; firing position as coastal defense. On the heights above the harbor, where our fighters were very busy at the airfield. From 1940-12-06 on, working as clerk at the motor-transport workshop (Schirrmeisterei). Christmas with roast pork, Stollen, and sparkling wine.
After a long trip even through Chemnitz, arrived in the Dobruja. In a former German village whose inhabitants have been resettled; Romanians from Macedonia are coming in. Mild spring weather.
South of the Danube mouth. Preparation for the next move: inoculations, tropical-fitness examination, lecture by the unit doctor. Checkers tournaments for eggs. Very bleak.

Arrived after travel via Silistra, Shumen and the 1100 m Vribiga/Varbitsa Pass. Billeted with a 90-year-old woman, the best quarters of the war. Heroes' Memorial Day with parade, Bulgarian church service.
Suddenly left Kotel, heartfelt farewell. Wonderful drive on partly breakneck roads through mountains, very hot and dusty in the Maritsa Valley.

place: Bulgarian border / from Sofia to NW
description: Beginning of the attack on Yugoslavia. From Sofia northwest on the march into Yugoslavia. To this day Wilhelm has not fired a shot.
First day in Yugoslavia, supporting the infantry off the main advance route. Hideous weather with rain and snow, winter gear brought back out.
Reached the Danube near Belgrade very quickly. Then a march back to the SW, destination unclear.
Location changed again. Orderly room in a sugar factory; real summer, lilacs in bloom. Vehicles are being painted for the long trip back to Germany.
The big southeastern journey at an end, moved into a large new barracks. No leave permitted, patrol duty, smoking ban. Marianne visits for a weekend despite trouble with the officer on duty.

place: In the Polish forest near Gleiwitz
description: First step back into the gypsy life. Slept in tents and on the vehicles in the forest; the jump from civilization to wilderness is very jarring.

After 4 days' march at the provisional destination. Schoolhouse as quarters; the town is 90% Jewish. Met many acquaintances from the Balkans; very miserable rations. Everyone knows it's going to be against Russia.

Beginning of the Russian campaign at 3:15 AM. Terrible artillery barrage on the east bank of the Bug — everything is burning. Wilhelm drives the Mercedes 170V of the chief Hptm. Baumann in the limber position.
After advancing with Panzer Group Kleist, 6 days before Ostrog, large Russian air attacks. On 06/28, Russian aircraft overhead from 4 AM to 8 PM. Stalin calls them the 'Ghost Division'.
A good week before Berdychiv; the Russians cover their retreat with heavy artillery. 3 days and nights of slow advance without pause.
After 4 days of real rest, back on the job. The old 11th Panzer Division has requested us. Russians in hopeless fighting under political commissars.
Drove 200 km without seeing the enemy, before Kryvyi Rih. The locals here are friendly, glad the Russians are gone. Air attack: 20 wounded in another battery during rifle inspection.
In firing position just before the Dnieper at Zaporizhzhia, 32°C in the shade, 40 in the sun. Many ethnic Germans who welcome us enthusiastically.
Took part in one of the hardest engagements. Afterward, pulled back about 100 km to a so-called rest position. Romanians and Italians are supposed to take over the front.

Took refuge in a wretched hut, front far away. Kiev fallen, 5000 civilian prisoners on the village square. Forced march 250 km east, broke through the Russian front. Bomb attack by Jak-2 with rockets; vehicle with half the steering wheel gone.
Slow march toward Rostov with a few rest stops, roughly north of Rostov. Russian night bombers sap the nerves.
Stuck in the mud — 3 km in 48 hours. Roads no longer exist; driving across the fields with tanks leading the way. 0°C at night on the vehicle, very uncomfortable.

In a German village where everything is in ruins, makeshift repairs. Static warfare. 8 days in one house with 2 NCOs and the residents (13 people, 25 total in 3 rooms). A Russian infant is being nursed, the children deloused.
About 40 km back, reoccupied old quarters. Time to sleep and wash. With the I-Staffel repairing vehicles; many signs of disintegration in the detachment. Frost since 11/12.

On the 2nd holiday, change of position to the Slovaks' sector, where the Russians were said to have broken through. Limber position in Uspenskaya. New Year's Eve spent with good food and 2 bottles of schnapps; Ivan drops a few bombs.

place: Firing position near Goldajewska (north of Rostov)
description: Limber position in a village behind the front, only 14 men, mostly Spieß and NCOs. Hare hunting, very quiet. 1 house for 14 men. Hptm. Baumann has since died from a severe wound.

For recuperation with the rear detachment. 3 days of travel by prime mover, rail, truck, and on foot. Quartered with an elderly couple in a Party-members' housing estate. Dentist, delousing, bathing, cinema. Market with sunflower oil, Turkish coffee.
Arrived at the battery in the evening, good rations with beefsteak and mashed potatoes. Winter won't let go — -35°C, lots of snow.
18-hour cross-country drive in our vehicle to Stalino for repair. Broken spring, tie rod bent four times. Hptm. Ertel takes Wilhelm along on leave in early May.
Wilhelm's first extended leave at home with Marianne and Helga since the start of the war.
Wilhelm spends some time in the barracks with the home detachment. Marianne visits with Helga.
After 2½ days of travel, quartered in a large barracks camp. Idling, cinema, baths. Marianne and Helga visit Wilhelm in mid-July. Second child (Stefan) on the way.
After leaving at 5 AM, settled comfortably in the train. Rations ample. In transport alone with a prime mover by rail heading east.
Been traveling through Russia for a while; today just before Dnipropetrovsk. Very warm.
Arrived in the evening, Hptm. Ertel at the station. The battalion is already off to its mission south of Rostov; Wilhelm set off with the prime mover to catch up.
First leg of the drive through summery countryside. Picked up 3 returning leave-takers from the battalion at the front assembly point.
Overnight. Cinema, soldiers' home, and swam in the Sea of Azov. Pleasantly warm. Tomorrow on through Rostov — the battalion is said to be 180 km further south already.
Reached the battalion; Wilhelm drives the prime mover with the supply echelon. Very lively welcome at the 2nd Battery from Winkler, Findewirth, and Bauer.
Reached Krasnodar as advance detachment; Kuban and Caucasus mountains in sight. Unbearable heat, in the evening the engine boils over. It can't be worse in Africa.
Slow going through hilly terrain. Russian infantry boys being beaten half to death; the heat crazy even at night; mosquitoes.
Only one mountain range separates us from Novorossiysk. Heard on the radio about the flag-raising on Mount Elbrus. Security watches at the front. Awarded the 'Winter Battle in the East' medal.
Sent with 10 comrades under Oberschirrmeister Braun to pick up prime movers. Cinema, concert, theater, sleeping in white sheets, bathing, buying melons.
Stuck, because the battalion is supposed to come through here. Schirrm. Braun to Rostov for spare parts.
Ran into the battery quite suddenly, now heading toward Maykop. A major attack on Tuapse is to be launched; the air crews are already preparing. Caviar at the Black Sea.

Living bunkers in the forest, jungle-like mountain terrain. Over 500 bunkers taken, field positions and tree obstacles breached. Terrain as steep as the Erzgebirge, guns must go down into the valley. Kam. Meinel killed on the road to Novorossiysk.
Prime mover broke down while fetching water, engine damage and front axle torn off. Struggled to Krasnodar, loaded onto rail to the army workshop in Rostov.
Here since 10/19 with the freight car, because there's no room inside Rostov. Diarrhea, fever and headache on the drafty prime mover.
Finally arrived here. In a new, undamaged factory. Wilhelm has to help with the repair himself, otherwise it will take weeks.
Arrived at J-Staffel with Hollstein, found mail waiting. Continues on to the column. 30 km on foot through mud and rain to the unit.

Back again from the front. Quarters on a straw mattress with the O.T. (Organisation Todt), electric light, cinema in the basement, old field kitchen. Christmas with roast pork, Stollen, sparkling wine, and red wine at the hotel.
Left the workshop in Rostov with Dauble's prime mover and a rear detachment. Ivan is said to already be in Rostov. Mail now takes a long time.
Ivan is roaming around with tanks too. A proper rear-area life with cinema and canteens. Comrades passing through. Quartered with an older woman, complete with knife, fork, plate, and cup.
Overnight in Zaporizhzhia on the way to the Crimea.
Ate at the soldiers' home. Rain and sleet.
Got rid of the vehicles right away. Theater and cinema, well fed at the soldiers' home. Next day 40 km back, where the battalion is being assembled.

place: Crimean village at the Yaila Mountains
description: With the company in a village in the middle of the steppe. The company consists mostly of men returning from leave, nearly all familiar faces. Waiting for the battalion to cross over.
Arrived at the battery after 4 days' march and crossing. Hilly terrain, cozy bunkers. Big welcome. Wilhelm has been Obergefreiter since 1942-12-01.

place: Kuban on the beach (Sea of Azov)
description: Position change to the beach, because Ivan has attempted a landing. Bauer's truck and billet burned down because of a carelessly tossed cigarette. Ate sturgeon and caviar.
Back in the old position in the mountains. Very hot, no more swimming. Tropical clothing issued. Spieß Karius transferred.
Wilhelm hit in the thigh by a submachine-gun round in an accident — about a 20 cm through-wound from below upward. Waiting to be transported back.
To the casualty collection point in Temryuk, from there the next day by hospital ship on a 20-hour crossing to Kerch.
After crossing the Crimea, onto the train. Hives from the tetanus shot. Field hospitals are being cleared for the coming offensive — hopefully back to the Reich.
Arrived at the field hospital. Leg is healing, big bandage gone, only two plasters left. Table tennis in the courtyard.
Transferred by hospital train to Vinnytsia. As of recently, recovered patients are sent to the replacement battalion. Surely back to Chemnitz.
Quartered with the convalescent company at Die Krone, spent 13 Sundays at home. Recruited by an officer for the rocket troops (V2) as prime-mover driver.
Overnight in Greifswald. Sleeping in a barn, very primitive, no lights. Gleaned potatoes, outing to the Baltic coast with coffee and cake.
Arrived at the barracks. Counted as replacement troops, less pay. A very mixed bunch, 18-50 years old. Written secrecy pledge. Theater, cinema, military concert.

With advance party to arrange quarters. Private billet with the Rathko family on Ad.-Mitl.-Str., attic room. The battery is to be formed here; a lot of accounting-clerk work. Christmas 1943 at home in Chemnitz.
On an 8-day detail, picking up a rocket at Peenemünde with a prime mover. Saw interesting things at the rocket test station.

place: Training grounds in the General Government (toward Białystok)
description: Went on detail, first with the paymaster in Köslin. Wilhelm as accounting clerk in barracks, with quartermaster, radio, cinema, soldiers' home. Practice firings over several hundred kilometers toward Białystok.
Returned via Leipzig, stopover in Berlin. In Stettin visited the firms Trompetter and Geck. Arrived at midnight, just in time for the birthday celebration with cake.

For practice firings, so couldn't go home for Günter's birth on 08/11. V2 practice rockets from Greifswalder Oie to 110 km altitude; Wasserfall rockets a few kilometers from the launch site. Accounting clerk and quartermaster for 180 men.
From Leipzig by express train to Hanover, then via Löhne, Hamm, Bielefeld, Münster. Waiting for the next train on a meadow outside town. Münster pretty well finished off.

V2 position further north than in 1939. Paymaster's office in a schoolhouse. Constant air-raid alarms. Two looting trips to Arnhem (Holland) after the English airborne attack. Guard of honor during a Speer visit at Schloss Vallar.

V2 is being fired at London from the Scheveningen pier. Lived in a villa in a park-like suburb, room with glass doors to the garden. Library with forbidden books (Mann, Feuchtwanger). Assigned as NS-Richtmann (National Socialist guidance officer).

place: Retreat via Rheine into the Lüneburg Heath
description: After the English crossed the Rhine at Wesel, the division was abruptly pulled out of Holland. March via Rheine eastward, heavy equipment blown up in the Lüneburg Heath.
Retreat as far as Lenzen. Along the way met the residents of Daber, who had been evacuated there. The unit is reconstituted as an artillery battery with 10.5 cm howitzers.
Sent marching toward Berlin and deployed at Fehrbellin against the Russians pushing toward Berlin. April passes with various engagements.

Hitler's death announced. V-2 leadership has reached an agreement with the Americans. Wilhelm promoted to Unteroffizier. The Americans are only looking for specialists. At 8 PM, handover to the Russians — so swam across the Elbe on truck inner tubes at 8°C.
By truck westward via Herford, 3 days and nights without roof or food on a sports field. Then loaded onto open freight cars, continuing toward Belgium.

Slipped away at the Krefeld suburban station with Kurt Günther and Gert Schrade. A little boy takes them along to his mother, who outfits them with work clothes. Night across country, then an estate with an opulent breakfast.
Through the innkeepers' mediation, and with money from the battery cash box, got hold of a boat; crossed the Rhine at Wittlaer unmolested.
From Hagen to Haspe by train. Big trouble at the ticket counter because of the foreign workers — mastered thanks to Schrade's English and French.

place: Trek through Sauerland / Westerwald / Vogelsberg / Rhön
description: 3-week hike with a small KAF atlas and compass along ridge trails. Overnight in barns, food begged for. Friendliness diminishes the further east they go.
Crossed the Werra with difficulty. The GI on the stone bridge was just busy with a German soldier in full uniform, so the three slipped past unnoticed.
Schrade splits off and goes to his uncle in Gotha. Wilhelm and Kurt Günther take the Autobahn as far as Chemnitz.
Stopped by a GI near Borna at the American/Russian demarcation line, talked their way out of it. Reached the Russian side via the railway bridge. On 05/28 in Röhrsdorf at the wife of comrade Birka.
Around 8 PM rang the bell at Stiftstr. 39. Helga still remembers: Papa had very dirty feet. Return after 6 years to civilian and family life.
